entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop used in the field experiment to assess phosphorus response.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS UPTAKE
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus absorbed by the plant.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A research study conducted in a field setting over four years from 1987 to 1990.

entities.name: ICRISAT FARM PATANCHERU
entities.description: The specific site where the field experiment was conducted.

entities.name: HYDERABAD
entities.description: The city near which the ICRISAT farm in Patancheru is located.

entities.name: RESIDUAL VALUE OF FERTILIZER PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The effectiveness of phosphorus fertilizer applied in a previous season on the subsequent crop.

entities.name: FERTILIZER PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: Phosphorus applied to the soil as a fertilizer.

entities.name: RAINFED CROPPING
entities.description: Agricultural practice relying on rainfall for water supply.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil characterized by high shrink-swell capacity.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A state where the soil lacks sufficient phosphorus for optimal plant growth.

entities.name: DRY MATTER YIELD RESPONSE
entities.description: The change in total biomass production in response to phosphorus application.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD RESPONSE
entities.description: The change in seed/grain production in response to phosphorus application.

entities.name: TOTAL PHOSPHORUS UPTAKE RESPONSE
entities.description: The change in total phosphorus absorbed by the plant in response to phosphorus application.
